feedback.text: As the practices targeted by the Digital Fairness Act are highly profitable for companies, I strongly believe that regulation is essential to protect consumers. I personally support all of the proposed measures. Like many others, I would also like to suggest that the complete and sudden disabling of a purchased video game should fall within the scope of this act. My reasoning is as follows: If an online game clearly indicated on its cover or store page that it would be terminated within two years, with no end-of-life plan and no way to play it afterward, I would likely choose not to purchase it. However, under current practices, it is possible to buy a game under exactly these conditions without any prior warning and I might unknowingly spend money on something I wouldnt have bought had I been informed. This strikes me as misleading. In my view, consumers should at least be informed of a game's lifespan before purchase. Alternatively, a requirement for a minimal end-of-life plan would make the industry even more transparent and sustainable.
